source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/CompositeAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- X11 SELF-COMPUTED structural smoke oracle. CompositeAnalysis (X5) fits one UnivariateAnalysis per child family over the shared dataset (seeded DEMCzs, iterations 100, output_length 200, thinning 1), then aggregates the children into a CompetingRisks composite frequency curve on the 5-point exceedance grid. The composite has NO MCMC of its own, so given the seeded child posteriors the aggregation is deterministic and reproduces bit-identically across C++/R/Python. curve_length == 5 is the deterministic length invariant. X12 tightens this to an exact composite-curve oracle against the real RMC.BestFit CompositeAnalysis via the emitter driver it must add.
